page: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the Dsv7 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor based on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The instance is configured with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in several compute and processing categories. The server achieves a top-tier Passmark CPU score of 105,569.39, with strong multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests. It demonstrates exceptional single-threaded and multi-threaded decompression capabilities, with bzip3 decompression reaching 556.8 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is strong, recording 345.73 GB/sec for large block read-only operations. In database and web serving benchmarks, the instance shows top-tier performance, processing 152.07 million Redis SET operations per second and delivering 124.31 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. Large language model prompt processing is strong, though text generation speeds remain average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96s_v7 offers high resource density and compute power for workloads requiring substantial parallel processing without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. The absence of local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale Premium Storage independently. This hardware profile is highly suited for multi-threaded enterprise applications, large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose server instance from the Dsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It features 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores), 384.0 GB of RAM, and a 480 MB L3 cache, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks reveal top-tier compute and decompression performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 105,569.39 and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression at 556.8 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth and top-tier Redis performance of 152.07 million operations per second. This configuration is optimized for high-throughput web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It delivers top-tier compute and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-core Intel-powered instance delivering exceptional multi-threaded compute performance and balanced memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB RAM powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
Top-tier multi-core performance with a Passmark CPU score of 105,569.39
Excellent decompression and database capabilities, processing 152.07 million Redis SET operations per second
Premium Storage capable configuration without bundled local storage or GPUs
Best suited for high-throughput web serving, database operations, and CPU-based inference
